channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Russian strike on Ukrainian town kills 11 as US cuts intelligence sharing with Kyiv
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — A Russian strike on a town in Ukraine's embattled Donetsk region killed at least 11 people, officials said Saturday, as a wave of heavy aerial attacks continued into the second night following a U.S. decision to stop sharing satellite images with Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: South Korea's impeached President Yoon released from prison
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea’s impeached conservative President Yoon Suk Yeol was released from prison on Saturday, a day after a Seoul court canceled his arrest to allow him to stand trial for rebellion without being detained.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Takeaways from the Gene Hackman and Betsy Arakawa deaths investigation
item.description: SANTA FE, N.M. (AP) — A towering figure in cinema for decades, retired actor Gene Hackman spent his final years in seclusion with his wife, living with heart disease and Alzheimer's, authorities said Friday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Venezuelan parents watch from home as their son is laid to rest in Texas after drowning
item.description: EAGLE PASS, Texas (AP) — The cries of Liliana Olivero watching from Venezuela through a cellphone pierced through the somber Christian music and gusts of wind as her son's casket was lowered into the ground.
item.dc:creator: VALERIE GONZALEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hawks, butterflies and a song for her murdered son help Georgia mom reclaim joy after killings
item.description: ROSWELL, Ga. (AP) — A hawk swooped in and landed on a tree, then watched as Michele and Jeremy Davis mourned their murdered son at a vigil outside his high school.
item.dc:creator: JEFF MARTIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Alabama minor is jailed with adults pre-trial in violation of federal law
item.description: Activists say an Alabama teenager's incarceration in an adult jail violates federal and state laws that require inmates who are minors to be separated from adults.
item.dc:creator: Safiyah Riddle, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Violent and sudden. What a firing squad execution looked like through my eyes
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — I've now watched through glass and bars as 11 men were put to death at a South Carolina prison. None of the previous 10 prepared me for watching the firing squad death of Brad Sigmon on Friday night. I might now be unique among U.S.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Expert tells judge to drop charges against NYC Mayor Eric Adams without letting them be refiled
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A judge has no choice but to grant the Justice Department’s unusual and divisive request to dismiss New York City Mayor Eric Adams’ corruption case, a court-appointed lawyer said Friday.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Late car payments hit highest level in decades
item.description: Americans are falling behind on their monthly car payments at the highest rate in more than 30 years.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: A car pulled from a river may tell what happened to an Oregon family of 5 that went missing in 1958
item.description: After two days of dredging, a crane on Friday pulled from the Columbia River a Ford station wagon that officials believe belonged to an Oregon family who disappeared while on a trip 66 years ago to find Christmas greenery.
item.dc:creator: MARTHA BELLISLE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Wendy’s to offer Frosty customization with sauces, mix-ins
item.description: Wendy’s will soon allow its customers to customize Frosty treats in what the company calls Frosty Swirls and Frosty Fusions. Unveiled during the company investor meeting on Thursday, Frosty Swirls will allow customers to add various sauces to a Classic Chocolate or Vanilla Frosty.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Tony! Toni! Tone! guitarist D'Wayne Wiggins dies
item.description: D'Wayne Wiggins, guitarist and founding member of Tony! Toni! Tone!, has died, according to a social media post from his family. The guitarist, who was a native of Oakland, had been battling bladder cancer.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Gene Hackman, Betsy Arakawa causes of death announced
item.description: The causes of death for actor Gene Hackman and his wife Betsy Arakawa were released Friday after days of mystery and speculation.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KTLA 5

item.title: California, other states suing Trump administration over mass firings of probationary federal workers
item.description: California and 19 other states are suing multiple federal agencies, contending President Donald Trump's administration has illegally fired thousands of federal probationary workers.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN WITTE, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Moon mission goes sideways: Private lunar lander declared dead after botched landing
item.description: A private lunar lander is no longer working after landing sideways in a crater near the moon’s south pole and its mission is over, officials said Friday.
item.dc:creator: By MARCIA DUNN AP Aerospace Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump threatens tariffs on Canadian dairy, lumber products as soon as Friday
item.description: Access to dairy and lumber markets have long been two of the biggest points of contention in the otherwise strong economic relationship between the U.S. and Canada.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Trump offers pathway to citizenship for South African farmers, families
item.description: President Trump has offered an expedited pathway to U.S. citizenship to South African farmers, calling their treatment in the country "terrible" and citing property laws that impose "unjust racial discrimination" against white Afrikaner farmers.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, WGN 9

item.title: Cheetos bags full of meth found at Indiana post office
item.description: Two Los Angeles residents have been arrested in southern Indiana after police found pounds of meth hidden inside Cheetos bags at a local post office.
item.dc:creator: Joe Schroeder, KRON4

item.title: 2 more arrested in theft of bulldog puppies from Colorado store
item.description: Deputies arrested the man who allegedly snatched a pair of bulldog puppies from an enclosure at a Centennial pet store.
item.dc:creator: Heather Willard, NewsNation

item.title: Canadian politician challenges Donald Trump Jr. to charity boxing match
item.description: Canadian Sen. Patrick Brazeau challenged Donald Trump Jr. to a charity boxing match over the “bogus” tariffs implemented this week by his father, President Trump. "I know, I still can't believe it myself.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KTLA 5

item.title: List of Social Security Administration offices DOGE is closing
item.description: The Department of Government Efficiency has started to make large budget cuts, resulting in the closure of multiple SSA offices.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, KRON4

item.title: Trump signs executive order creating bitcoin reserve
item.description: Trump, who once called crypto a "scam," has drastically changed his tune on the subject and promised on the campaign trail to make the U.S. the "crypto capital of the world.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Pope Francis hits 3-week mark of hospitalization
item.description: Francis offered a first public sign of just how weak he is on Thursday by recording an audio message that was broadcast to the faithful in St. Peter’s Square.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: WATCH: Florida residents record SpaceX explosion
item.description: Residents in Tampa Bay, Florida were confounded after seeing what seemed to be an explosion in the sky around Thursday's sunset.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Rodriguez, KTLA 5

item.title: Medical plane’s voice recorder likely wasn’t working for years before Philadelphia crash
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — The cockpit voice recorder was not working on a medical transport plane that killed seven people when it plummeted into a Philadelphia neighborhood in January and likely had not been functioning for several years, the National Transportation Safety Board said in a preliminary rep
item.dc:creator: MARYCLAIRE DALE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump says he’s 'looking at' revoking protected status for Ukrainians
item.description: “We're not looking to hurt anybody, and we're certainly not looking to hurt them,” Trump told reporters in the Oval Office.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: These national parks saw record attendance in 2024, but they may not tell you about it
item.description: The National Park Service has reportedly been instructed not to draw attention to the record attendance amid staffing cuts across the agency.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KRON4

item.title: New Mexico adult dies with measles, though cause of death isn't yet confirmed
item.description: An adult who was infected with measles has died in New Mexico, state health officials announced Thursday, though the virus has not been confirmed as the cause. The person who died was unvaccinated and did not seek medical care, a state health department spokesperson said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: DEVI SHASTRI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: President Trump delays some tariffs on Mexico and Canada
item.description: President Donald Trump Thursday paused the 25% tariffs on Canada and Mexico that started earlier this week, with plans to restart them in April.
item.dc:creator: Maddie Biertempfel, KTLA 5

item.title: Andrew Tate says he plans to live in Florida despite 'hee haw' over return to US soil amid overseas charges
item.description: Andrew Tate says he plans to buy a Florida penthouse and live in the United States as he awaits trial in Romania on sex trafficking and other charges.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A fifth of US butterflies have disappeared since 2000: Study
item.description: Butterflies are disappearing from the United States at an alarming rate, with their total population declining by more than a fifth over two decades, a new study has found.
item.dc:creator: Sharon Udasin, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Ghost preparers and spear phishing: Here's the tax scams to avoid
item.description: With the tax filing deadline coming up on April 15, the IRS released its annual "Dirty Dozen" list of tax scams for 2025 to warn people of common schemes.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, Fox 5

item.title: Roy Ayers, 'Everybody Loves the Sunshine' musician, passes away at 84
item.description: Los Angeles native, Roy Ayers, who was behind the popular song "Everybody Loves the Sunshine" has passed away. He was 84 years old.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, KRON4

item.title: New Google feature flags when you might be getting scammed in real time
item.description: Google is rolling out new, AI-powered tools to help stop scammers in their tracks, before they get your data or money.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, Fox 5

item.title: As many top Democrats stay silent on Cuomo mayoral run, an accuser feels betrayed
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Lindsey Boylan helped drive Andrew Cuomo from office in 2021 when the former aide came forward as the first woman to accuse the governor of sexual harassment.
item.dc:creator: BY JAKE OFFENHARTZ and ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Scientists raise concerns as the US stops sharing air quality data from embassies worldwide
item.description: NEW DELHI (AP) — The U.S. government will stop sharing air quality data gathered from its embassies and consulates, worrying local scientists and experts who say the effort was vital to monitor global air quality and improve public health.
item.dc:creator: TAIWO ADEBAYO and SIBI ARASU, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Baja Midnight, Quesocrisp Taco, and more: What’s coming to Taco Bell in 2025
item.description: Taco Bell announced a slate of new menu items and plans for the fast food chain at its second annual Live Mas event, which took place in New York City on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, WFLA, NBC
